findings.reason: PMID 34755828 attached to IMpower-010 resolves on PubMed to 'Corrigendum: Human platelet lysate biotherapy for traumatic brain injury' (Brain 2021); unrelated to atezolizumab adjuvant NSCLC. Trial NCT02486718 is real IMpower-010 but pmid is wrong.
findings.ground_truth_hint: IMpower-010 primary publication PMID 34555333 (Felip et al, Lancet 2021)

findings.reason: PMID 29610497 attached to NICCO resolves to 'Use of a Laparoscopic Bag for Endoscopic Retrieval of Gastric Tumor' (Am J Gastroenterol 2018); unrelated to neoadjuvant nivolumab melanoma. NCT02434354 also requires verification.
findings.ground_truth_hint: NICCO/OpACIN PMID likely 29403949 (Blank et al, Nat Med 2018)

findings.reason: PMID 36648215 attached to 'IMmuNED' resolves to Cochrane Database Syst Rev 2023 'Neoadjuvant treatment for stage III and IV cutaneous melanoma' (a SR, not a primary trial). 'IMmuNED' trial name also not verifiable; possible fabricated-trial.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Cochrane SR PMID 36648215 is a review article, not a primary RCT

findings.reason: PMID 18567918 attached to 'OPUS-2' (claimed 2024) resolves to 'Cilostazol on platelet function... DM/CAD' (Eur Heart J 2008); entirely unrelated and the PMID is from 2008 while trial year is 2024 — also year-pmid-era-mismatch.
findings.ground_truth_hint: PMID 18567918 is a 2008 cardiology cilostazol paper; trial name/NCT need verification

findings.reason: NCT identifier has invalid trailing 'b' suffix; ClinicalTrials.gov NCT IDs are NCT followed by 8 digits only. Likely fabricated or copy-paste-suffixed.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Strip trailing 'b' and verify NCT04449003 on CT.gov; trial 'MERIT-HCV' has no canonical referent

findings.reason: COPE-Liver (Nasralla) row has publishedHR=-50.0 with hrLCI=-65, hrUCI=-35 and estimandType=MD, but tE/tN/cE/cN all null. An MD of -50 for a transplant graft-injury outcome is implausibly extreme and inconsistent with the published COPE-Liver primary endpoint (peak AST log-MD ~ -0.5 units). Effect-size implausibility + missing arm counts.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Nasralla NEJM 2018 PMID 29670272 reports peak AST as log-transformed MD ~-0.45, not -50

findings.reason: PMID 37977660 attached to PACE-A resolves to Neuron 2023 'Differential Requirement for Plexin-A3/A4 in Sensory Neurons'; unrelated to prostate SBRT. Also NCT02546050 is not the canonical PACE-A registration (PACE-A is part of NCT01584258 PACE program with sub-comparisons).
findings.ground_truth_hint: PACE-A primary publication van As et al, Lancet Oncol 2024 PMID 38824936; verify NCT mapping

findings.reason: PMID 36794895 attached to MIRAGE resolves to Acta Derm Venereol 'A Light-red Nodule on the Jaw: A Quiz'; entirely unrelated to MR-LINAC vs CBCT prostate SBRT. NCT04557098 is also flagged because it conflicts with NCT04557099 used elsewhere in the chunk (MajesTEC-1) — duplicate-adjacent-NCT pattern.
findings.ground_truth_hint: MIRAGE primary publication Kishan et al, JAMA Oncol 2023 PMID 36633858

findings.reason: PMID 27339115 (CHHIP) correctly resolves to Dearnaley et al Lancet Oncol 2016 hypofractionated prostate RT 5-yr outcomes — internally consistent. No defect; recorded as positive control.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Confirmed correct PMID

findings.reason: MENSA has cE=191 and cN=191 — control event count equals control N (100% exacerbation rate), schema-incoherent (events cannot equal N for an exacerbation outcome). PMID 25199059 resolves correctly to Ortega et al NEJM 2014 MENSA (HR confirms 0.47 reduction), so this is a tE/cE/cN data corruption, not a trial-identity issue.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Real MENSA reports ~290 vs 388 exacerbations in mepo vs placebo arms; cN=191 is wrong (likely value-swap)

findings.reason: QUEST dupilumab row: tN=631 vs cN=313 — actual QUEST randomized 1902 (1264 dupi + 638 placebo) so this is ~half the trial; tE/cE 175/293 also internally implausible (cE 293 of cN 313 = 93.6% event rate, while tE/tN=175/631=27.7%; ratio inverted vs RR=0.52).
findings.ground_truth_hint: Castro et al NEJM 2018 QUEST PMID 29782224; cE and cN appear swapped

findings.reason: ZONDA OR=4.12 labeled OR; computed OR from tE=50/tN=73, cE=18/cN=75 is ~6.87 — disagrees with published ZONDA OR for OCS reduction. cE/tE counts likely off.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Nair et al NEJM 2017 ZONDA PMID 28530840; verify ZONDA OCS-reduction OR and arm counts

findings.reason: MIRRA EGPA row has PMID 28528561 which resolves to NEJM 2017 'Angiotensin II for Vasodilatory Shock' (ATHOS-3); completely unrelated to mepolizumab/EGPA. The NCT02596542 + trial name MIRRA + group string are otherwise plausible.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Wechsler et al NEJM 2017 MIRRA PMID 28514605 (correct PMID for mepolizumab in EGPA)

findings.reason: SKYLIGHT-1 fezolinetant row has PMID 37178735 which resolves to Gastroenterology 2023 'Enterochromaffin Cells: Small in Number but Big in Impact'; unrelated to NK3R hot-flash therapy. Effect estimate (-2.39 MD) is plausible for SKYLIGHT-1.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Lederman et al Lancet 2023 SKYLIGHT-1 PMID 36924777

findings.reason: ONSET-2 (varenicline NS Tyrvaya) row has PMID 33823288 which resolves to 'Malaria in urban Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia' (Travel Med Infect Dis 2021); unrelated to dry-eye/varenicline. Band is MANUAL_REVIEW — flagging as new HIGH-severity evidence beyond band designation.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Wirta et al Cornea 2022 ONSET-2 PMID likely 35588194

findings.reason: OPUS-1 lifitegrast row has PMID 26022308 which resolves to Folia Primatol 'Antipredator Vocalization Usage in Male Ring-Tailed Lemur'; unrelated to ophthalmology. Already MANUAL_REVIEW band but this is additional HIGH evidence.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Sheppard et al Ophthalmology 2014 OPUS-1 PMID 24216057

findings.reason: PROTECT-AF row has PMID 21183140 resolving to Lancet Infect Dis 2011 'Fighting bacterial resistance at the root: need for adapted EMEA guidelines'; entirely unrelated to LAAO/atrial fibrillation. MANUAL_REVIEW band — flagging as new evidence.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Holmes et al Lancet 2009 PROTECT-AF PMID 19683639 (or 24682348 for 2014 long-term follow-up)

findings.reason: XPORT-MF selinexor row has PMID 39911057 which resolves to Future Oncol 2025 'Selinexor plus ruxolitinib in JAK inhibitor treatment-naive myelofibrosis: SENTRY Phase 3 study design'. This is the SENTRY trial design paper, NOT the XPORT-MF readout. Trial-name/PMID mismatch within same molecule (selinexor-ruxolitinib) but wrong study.
findings.ground_truth_hint: XPORT-MF-034 primary publication Tantravahi et al not yet matched; SENTRY is a different (phase 3) trial

findings.reason: PERSIST-1 row: cE=0, cN=107, tE=41, tN=220 produces OR=39.51 with UCI=643.06. While numerically consistent with a zero-cell, an OR of 39.5 with CI ranging to 643 indicates the data were not corrected (no continuity correction applied) and the precision is uninterpretable — flag as effect-size-implausibility/extreme-sparse.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Mascarenhas et al JAMA Oncol 2018 PERSIST-1 reports more granular spleen-volume response with continuity correction

findings.reason: Row claims TANGO-I with NCT02168946; but NCT02168946 is actually TANGO-II (meropenem-vaborbactam, CRE infections). TANGO-I (cUTI) is NCT02166476. The HAP_VAP review later in this chunk reuses NCT02168946 for 'TANGO-II', confirming the swap.
findings.ground_truth_hint: TANGO-I = NCT02166476 (Kaye et al JAMA 2018 PMID 29560602); TANGO-II = NCT02168946

findings.reason: APEKS-NP row uses NCT02714595, but NCT02714595 is CREDIBLE-CR (cefiderocol vs BAT, carbapenem-resistant), which is correctly listed in CARBAPENEM_RESISTANT review. Real APEKS-NP = NCT03032380.
findings.ground_truth_hint: APEKS-NP NCT03032380 (Wunderink et al Lancet Infect Dis 2021)

findings.reason: REPRISE row uses NCT01953419, but real REPRISE (ceftazidime-avibactam) is NCT01644643 (correctly listed in CARBAPENEM_RESISTANT review). NCT01953419 is an unrelated trial. PMID 27108754 also resolves to 'Stress Health' commentary, unrelated.
findings.ground_truth_hint: REPRISE NCT01644643, Carmeli et al Lancet Infect Dis 2016 PMID 27139491

findings.reason: ASPECT-NP ceftolozane-tazo row has PMID 31203930 which resolves to Travel Med Infect Dis 'Adverse events following purified chick embryo cell rabies vaccine in VAERS'; unrelated to pneumonia antibiotic. NCT02070757 is real ASPECT-NP.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Kollef et al Lancet Infect Dis 2019 ASPECT-NP PMID 31000395

findings.reason: PRODIGE-7 row has PMID 33387458 which resolves to Am J Med 2020 'The Philosopher-Priest-Scientist and the New Age of Medicine'; entirely unrelated to HIPEC colorectal trial.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Quenet et al Lancet Oncol 2021 PRODIGE-7 PMID 33485731

findings.reason: GASTROCHIP row has PMID 32945916 which resolves to Pathologe 2020 'Quality assurance in diagnostic in situ hybridization (QuIP)' (German pathology paper); unrelated to gastric HIPEC.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Glehen et al JAMA Surg 2021 GASTROCHIP PMID 33688930

findings.reason: OVHIPEC-1 row with PMID 29342393 correctly resolves to van Driel et al NEJM 2018 'Hyperthermic Intraperitoneal Chemotherapy in Ovarian Cancer' (HR 0.66 matches). Recorded as positive control.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Confirmed correct PMID and HR

findings.reason: POLISH-II row has PMID 27298274 which resolves to BMJ 2016 news brief 'Air pollution is a leading risk factor for stroke'; entirely unrelated to short-course RT rectal cancer trial.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Bujko et al Ann Oncol 2016 POLISH-II PMID 26884592

findings.reason: STELLAR row has PMID 36206146 which resolves to Comput Inform Nurs 'Explainable Artificial Intelligence for Predicting Hospital-Acquired Pressure Injuries in COVID-19'; unrelated to rectal cancer TNT trial.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Jin et al J Clin Oncol 2022 STELLAR PMID 35649231

findings.reason: DISRUPT-CAD-3 row has PMID 33176145 which resolves to Cell Reports 'Dopamine Inputs from VTA into mPFC Modulate Neuropathic Pain-Associated Behaviors in Mice'; entirely unrelated to coronary intravascular lithotripsy.
findings.ground_truth_hint: Hill et al J Am Coll Cardiol 2020 DISRUPT-CAD-3 PMID 33049859

findings.reason: DISRUPT-PAD-3 row: tE=153 with tN=153 implies 100% event rate in the treatment arm (impossible for any clinically meaningful outcome), cE=90/cN=153. RR=1.82 and CI tight (1.55-2.13) suggest data are summary of procedural success not a clinical event — schema labeled RR with implausible 100% rate.
findings.ground_truth_hint: DISRUPT-PAD-3 reports angiographic success; reframe outcome or correct tE which should be < tN

findings.reason: ACMS (lap distal pancreatectomy China) listed with NCT01988116 registered 2013 and year=2020. Plausible registration-to-publication lag, but verify NCT-acronym binding (ACMS is not a standard public acronym for a single lap-pancreatectomy RCT).
findings.ground_truth_hint: Verify ACMS acronym against AACT — possible fabricated acronym

findings.reason: MajesTEC-1 (teclistamab) row uses NCT04557099, but real MajesTEC-1 = NCT04557098. The adjacent +1 NCT digit is a classic typo/fabrication signature, and NCT04557098 is also misused as 'MIRAGE' earlier in this chunk (SBRT_PROSTATE review) — pointing to systematic NCT-recycling. Band is QUARANTINED; this is new HIGH evidence.
findings.ground_truth_hint: MajesTEC-1 = NCT04557098, Moreau et al NEJM 2022 PMID 35947710

findings.reason: MonumenTAL-1 (talquetamab) listed with NCT04634552; real MonumenTAL-1 = NCT03399799 (phase 1) / NCT04634552 is MonumenTAL-2 or a related program — verify. Already QUARANTINED, recorded as new HIGH evidence.
findings.ground_truth_hint: MonumenTAL-1 NCT03399799, Chari et al NEJM 2022 PMID 38507751 (combined readout)

review_verdicts.summary: 9/10 trials have plausible NCT-name-year-arm structure; 1 row (IMpower-010 NCT02486718) has pmid-unrelated 34755828. Effect sizes and arm counts internally consistent; the chunk's most-trustworthy review.

3 of 10 trials carry pmid-unrelated annotations (NICCO 29610497, IMmuNED 36648215=SR not RCT, OPUS-2 18567918=2008 cardiology paper); IMmuNED also possible fabricated trial. Re-extract PMIDs for entire review.
MERIT-HCV row has invalid NCT04449003b (trailing 'b'); THINKER NCT03208244 + PMID 30326012 plausible. Single-arm structure of all three trials limits assertions.
Single trial ATTRibute-CM NCT03860935 with HR 0.78 and arm counts consistent with published 2024 NEJM readout; estimandType null is a schema-laxity flag but not a fabrication signal.
5/6 trials plausible; COPE-Liver row has impossible MD=-50 with all arm counts null — schema-sanity violation suggesting either unit-error or extraction failure.
PACE-A (NCT02546050 + PMID 37977660 unrelated) and MIRAGE (PMID 36794895 unrelated, also NCT shared with MajesTEC-1) both flagged HIGH; remainder plausible. CHHIP/PROFIT/RTOG-0415 likely real.
MENSA cE=cN=191 schema-incoherent; QUEST cN=313 (real 638), ZONDA OR/arms inconsistent. Three of 12 trials have arm-count corruption — fix tE/cE/cN extraction across whole review.
OVHIPEC-1 correctly cited (PMID 29342393 = NEJM 2018 ✓); PRODIGE-7 (PMID 33387458 Am J Med editorial) and GASTROCHIP (PMID 32945916 Pathologe QA paper) both pmid-unrelated. Re-extract PMIDs.
TANGO-I is mapped to NCT02168946 which is actually TANGO-II — NCT-acronym swap. Other 7 trials look NCT-plausible but most PMIDs are null, limiting verification.
Single trial MIRRA — NCT02596542 correct, but PMID 28528561 resolves to ATHOS-3 angiotensin-II shock paper. Effect size HR 2.27 plausible for steroid-free remission.
SKYLIGHT-1 PMID 37178735 unrelated; other 4 rows have null PMIDs but plausible MD effect sizes for fezolinetant/elinzanetant. Re-extract PMIDs.

NCT-name swaps for APEKS-NP (uses CREDIBLE-CR's NCT02714595) and REPRISE (NCT01953419 unrelated, PMID 27108754 unrelated Stress Health paper); ASPECT-NP PMID 31203930 = rabies vaccine VAERS letter. Three of six rows have HIGH NCT/PMID defects.
Already MANUAL_REVIEW; new HIGH evidence: XPORT-MF cited with PMID 39911057 = SENTRY trial design paper (wrong study, same molecule); PERSIST-1 sparse-zero produces OR=39.51 with CI to 643.
Already MANUAL_REVIEW; all 4 BTK-inhibitor trials (Evobrutinib EVOLUTIONRMS, Tolebrutinib HERCULES/GEMINI-1) have plausible NCTs, arm counts, and HR; PMIDs are empty but no fabrication evidence.
Already MANUAL_REVIEW; PROTECT-AF PMID 21183140 = Lancet Infect Dis EMEA-guidelines paper (HIGH new evidence). Other 5 NCTs plausible.

Already MANUAL_REVIEW; ONSET-2 PMID 33823288 (malaria Kuala Lumpur) and OPUS-1 PMID 26022308 (lemur vocalizations) both wildly unrelated; OPUS-2/OPUS-3 have MD effects that don't match RR-labeled siblings — re-extract entire review.
Already MANUAL_REVIEW; LEOPARD-2/PADULAP/LAPOP/DIPLOMA all plausible NCTs and arm counts; ACMS acronym uncertain but other markers consistent.
Already MANUAL_REVIEW; DISRUPT-CAD-3 PMID 33176145 (mouse VTA-dopamine paper) is HIGH-severity pmid-unrelated. DISRUPT-PAD-3 schema flagged (tE=tN=153).
Already MANUAL_REVIEW; POLISH-II PMID 27298274 (air-pollution news brief) and STELLAR PMID 36206146 (AI for pressure-injury prediction in ICU) both unrelated. Other 6 trials plausible.

Already QUARANTINED; new HIGH evidence: MajesTEC-1 listed with NCT04557099 (real = NCT04557098, same wrong digit reused as MIRAGE NCT elsewhere); MonumenTAL-1 NCT04634552 is likely a sibling-program ID, not the actual trial. All 3 rows single-arm with cE=cN=0, consistent with FULL_FABRICATION band.

Already QUARANTINED; chunk holds 10 rows with all-null PMIDs and effect sizes implausibly tight (5 RR values between 2.04-2.71 with narrow CIs across distinct molecules and regions — looks generated). No new PubMed-verifiable PMIDs to spot-check; band designation retained.
